{{Short description="Basic forces of nature from which all other forces can be derived}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Fundamental interaction
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Force]], [[Particle physics]], [[Standard Model]], [[Gravity]]
}}

In [[physics]], the '''fundamental interactions''' (also called '''fundamental forces''') are the most basic forces from which all other interactions can be derived. There are four conventionally accepted fundamental interactions:

# '''[[Strong interaction]]''' — binds [[quark]]s into [[hadron]]s; holds [[atomic nucleus|nuclei]] together
# '''[[Electromagnetism]]''' — acts between [[electric charge|electrically charged particles]]
# '''[[Weak interaction]]''' — responsible for [[beta decay]] and [[neutrino]] interactions
# '''[[Gravity]]''' — attraction between masses; described by [[general relativity]]

== Comparison ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Interaction !! Relative strength !! Range (m) !! Mediator !! Acts on
|-
| [[Strong interaction|Strong]] || 1 || ~10⁻¹⁵ || [[Gluon]]s || [[Quark]]s, [[gluon]]s
|-
| [[Electromagnetism|Electromagnetic]] || ~10⁻² || Infinite || [[Photon]] || [[Electric charge|Charged particles]]
|-
| [[Weak interaction|Weak]] || ~10⁻⁶ || ~10⁻¹⁸ || [[W and Z bosons]] || [[Quark]]s, [[lepton]]s
|-
| [[Gravity|Gravitational]] || ~10⁻³⁸ || Infinite || [[Graviton]] (hypothetical) || [[Mass]]/[[Energy]]
|}

== Unification ==
A major goal of theoretical physics is to unify all fundamental interactions:

* '''[[Electroweak theory]]''' — unified description of [[electromagnetism]] and [[weak interaction]] (confirmed by discovery of [[W and Z bosons]], 1983)
* '''[[Grand Unified Theory]] (GUT)''' — unifies strong, weak, and electromagnetic interactions (theoretical, unconfirmed)
* '''[[Theory of everything]] (TOE)''' — would additionally include [[gravity]]; [[string theory]] is a leading candidate

== Historical unification ==
* [[Isaac Newton]] unified terrestrial and celestial gravity (1687)
* [[James Clerk Maxwell]] unified electricity, magnetism, and light (1861)
* [[Sheldon Glashow]], [[Abdus Salam]], [[Steven Weinberg]] unified electromagnetic and weak interactions (1960s)

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Electromagnetism]]
* [[Gravity]]
* [[Standard Model]]
* [[Strong interaction]]
* [[Weak interaction]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Fundamental interactions| ]]
[[Category:Concepts in physics]]
[[Category:Particle physics]]
